examples: Every family cultivates its vendetta; every clan, its feud.
To deal with the rabbit problem, settlers introduced stoats — a member of the weasel clan and a terrific predator.
A weapon which would kill with accuracy at fifteen hundred yards opened a whole new vista of delights to every family or clan which could acquire it.
But the Sainsbury clan has taken some unusual steps to improve its share price.
At half-day-long meetings, the entire Quinn clan pores over financial reports and meets with accountants and tax attorneys.
